- [ ] [registry-separation-rule] `.agents/skill_registry.json` :: Make the public rule explicit: host-native for decide, interpret, shape, critique, route, and supervise; kernel-backed for bounded deterministic execution primitives. | scan: hall-scan:1774733854491 | baseline: 3.97 | contracts: AGENTS.qmd, ARCHITECTURE.md | acceptance: Registry entries that represent public cognitive skills are classified agent-native. Kernel executor weaves remain available as bounded implementation primitives. Dispatcher and bootstrap assumptions remain aligned with the split.

- [ ] [chant-public-host-front] `.agents/skill_registry.json` :: Chant is a public planning surface. - [?] [bead:chant-session:611c8d03-c6a6-43aa-950f-5eb4bfbdaa8d:bead-oracle-provider-override] `src/node/core/commands/oracle.ts` :: The shared host bridge already supports explicit provider injection via `requestHostText({ provider })`, so the smallest correct improvement is to expose that capability through the direct host-sampling spoke only. This preserves current global env-based resolution by default while letting operators force `codex`, `gemini`, or `claude` for a single call. The change should stay confined to the CLI command option parsing and the existing handoff path into the host bridge, avoiding broader provider-resolution refactors. | scan: hall-scan:1774359037978 | baseline: 6.76 | contracts: src/core/host_intelligence.ts, tests/unit/test_oracle_command.test.ts, tests/unit/test_host_intelligence.test.ts | acceptance: The `oracle` command accepts a `--provider <gemini|codex|claude>` override for a single invocation. | The command layer rejects invalid provider values before sampling or bridge dispatch. | Both direct prompt execution and `--db` synapse fulfillment forward the forced provider through `OracleCommandOptions` and sampling flow into the host bridge. | The shared host bridge handoff uses the explicit provider when supplied, even if environment variables indicate a different provider. | Focused TypeScript tests for the oracle command and host bridge pass. | triage: Paused for live fire test

- [-] [bead:chant-session:6908a6c1-1be0-42f2-8412-a6b46e4f06a0:bead:autobot-preflight:semantics-contract] `src/core/engine/autobot_skill.py` :: Once the module imports, the next risk is incorrect behavior. The current preflight branch finalizes whenever `run_preflight_checker()` returns a `CommandResult`, even when the checker failed or timed out, and `persist_checker_validation()` dereferences Hermes-only fields although the preflight path passes no Hermes result. This bead keeps the change bounded to the AutoBot worker and validation contract. | scan: hall-scan:1774359037978 | baseline: 6.76 | contracts: tests/unit/test_autobot_skill.py | acceptance: `run_bead_mode()` resolves early only when the preflight checker result succeeded. | A failed or timed-out preflight run falls through into the existing Hermes retry loop without changing that loop's behavior. | Checker validation persistence accepts the preflight path without requiring Hermes-only metadata, or an equivalent dedicated preflight persistence path is introduced. | A successful preflight resolution records `attempt_count == 0` and resolves the bead without launching Hermes. | note: Archived to isolate the 2026-03-19 monitored live-fire session.
